channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Plan to relocate Ibaka Deep Seaport from its natural harbour
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Explore the controversial plan to relocate the Ibaka Deep Seaport from its naturally deep harbour in Akwa Ibom, raising concerns among stakeholders. Learn
Read More: https://punchng.com/plan-to-relocate-ibaka-deep-seaport-from-its-natural-harbour/

item.title: Inflation still high despite latest decline, OPS warns
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Despite a marginal decline to 15.91% in June 2026, Nigeria’s inflation remains high, warns the Organised Private Sector. Food prices continue to rise.
Read More: https://punchng.com/inflation-still-high-despite-latest-decline-ops-warns/

item.title: PIA permits Dangote’s dollar fuel sales – Regulator
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s petroleum regulator confirms Dangote Refinery can sell fuel in dollars, citing PIA provisions for cost recovery. Learn how this impacts marketers
Read More: https://punchng.com/pia-permits-dangotes-dollar-fuel-sales-regulator/

item.title: Wema Bank deepens fintech partnerships to drive digital finance
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Wema Bank reinforces its commitment to fintech partnerships, driving digital finance growth and innovation across Nigeria’s financial services ecosystem.
Read More: https://punchng.com/wema-bank-deepens-fintech-partnerships-to-drive-digital-finance/

item.title: Lasaco secures N19.3bn in oversubscribed rights issue
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lasaco Assurance Plc successfully raised N19.3bn in an oversubscribed rights issue, demonstrating strong investor confidence and boosting its financial cap
Read More: https://punchng.com/lasaco-secures-n19-3bn-in-oversubscribed-rights-issue/

item.title: Sanwo-Olu to declare AAAN’s 53rd AGM open
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos Governor Sanwo-Olu will declare open the AAAN’s 53rd AGM in Lagos, discussing AI, digital ecosystems, and the future of advertising.
Read More: https://punchng.com/sanwo-olu-to-declare-aaans-53rd-agm-open/

item.title: Reps demand beneficiaries of N34tn Customs duty waivers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives Committee on Finance has directed the Nigeria Customs Service to provide a comprehensive breakdown of N34tn import duty waiver
Read More: https://punchng.com/reps-demand-beneficiaries-of-n34tn-customs-duty-waivers/

item.title: US inflation eases in June on lower energy costs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
US wholesale inflation eased in June 2026 due to falling energy costs, government data shows. Discover how this impacts US inflation and future economic ou
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-inflation-eases-in-june-on-lower-energy-costs/

item.title: Economic reforms delivering steady growth – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Bola Tinubu states Nigeria’s economic reforms are delivering steady growth despite challenges. Deloitte Africa commends the administration’s fina
Read More: https://punchng.com/economic-reforms-delivering-steady-growth-tinubu/

item.title: FG backs artisans with N3.6bn skills devt fund
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has approved N3.6bn for the Skill Up Artisans Programme, supporting tailors and strengthening Nigeria’s informal sector.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-backs-artisans-with-n3-6bn-skills-devt-fund/

item.title: Tuchel vows to remain as England head coach after Argentina defeat
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Thomas Tuchel says he will stay on to lead England into Euro 2028 despite their World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina.

item.title: BEFORE WE DISMEMBER NYSC
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: MONDAY PHILIPS EKPE writes that Nigeria’s national youth service needs well-considered reforms, not capricious changes Federal Government’s recent pronouncement of its plan to reform the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) has

item.title: Senate gives NNPC auditors one week to explain N210trn unreconciled figures
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senate Public Accounts Committee (SPAC) yesterday gave the external auditors of the Nigerian National Petroleum Company PLC (NNPC PLC.) one week to produce documents explaining more than N210 trillion recorded as receivables and payables in the company’s audited financial statements, warning that fi

item.title: IMF, President Tinubu and the transparency challenge
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Moshood Oshunfurewa If we are to reincarnate the late Chief Gani Oyesola Fawehinmi (1938–2009), a legendary Nigerian human rights lawyer, publisher, author, social critic, and Senior Advocate of the Masses (SAM), on the current budget imbroglio, 2% of GDP, and Gbaja/Adeyemi scandal, I might not m

item.title: NFF: Need for a new beginning
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria’s absence from the 2026 World Cup is not fate. It is the bill finally arriving for years of reckless housekeeping at the Nigeria Football Federation (NFF), and those responsible must be made to pay for it. Our football history shows that Nigeria rarely loses to better teams. It loses to itse

item.title: US asks FG to do more to protect Christians from attacks
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Government of the United States has asked the Federal Government to do more to protect Christians in the country from attacks. The post US asks FG to do more to protect Christians from attacks appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: From Lafarge to HBM: A New Identity to Power Nigeria’s Next Phase of Industrial Growth
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: After decades of operating under one of the most recognisable names in Nigeria’s building materials industry, Lafarge Africa Plc has entered a new era with its transition to HBM Nigeria

item.title: Goodluck Jonathan: Nigeria’s most cowardly politician! By Olu Fasan
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Twice in recent presidential election cycles, Goodluck Jonathan, Nigeria’s former one-term president, allowed speculations to spread like wildfire that he would seek the presidency again. But twice, without as much as a whimper, the speculations fizzled out into thin air like a puff of wind. Recentl

item.title: Oyo abduction and Makinde’s call for UN probe, by Ikechukwu Amaechi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: There is no well-meaning Nigerian who is not relieved that the Oyo State kidnap saga ended happily despite the eight fatalities and the 56 agonising days it took to embrace the light at the end of what was, to all intents and purposes, a very dark chapter in Nigeria’s beleaguered history. The mass a

item.title: FG, NDDC mobilise $500m Niger Delta agric fund to galvanize food production
item.dc:creator: Vanguard

item.title: Abbas calls for stronger intelligence-sharing, improved border security
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Speaker of the House of Representatives, Abbas Tajudeen, has called for stronger intelligence-sharing, improved technology, safer schools, enhanced border security and better welfare for security personnel as part of efforts to address Nigeria’s security challenges. The post Abbas calls for stronger

item.title: The high cost of condescension: Why workplace feedback needs dignity upgrade, by Ruth Oji
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Imagine this scenario: a senior manager publicly interrogates a junior employee about a minor reporting error. “Did you even think before submitting this?” he asked, voice dripping with contempt, as colleagues shifted uncomfortably in their seats. “What exactly goes through your mind when you do suc

item.title: Ganduje stresses need for ideology-based politics
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Kano State governor, Abdullahi Ganduje, has stressed the need for ideology-based political parties in Nigeria, saying the absence of clear ideological foundations has weakened the country’s democratic process. The post Ganduje stresses need for ideology-based politics appeared first on The Su

item.title: Multiple projects in Imo justify fuel subsidy removal – Presidency
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Presidency has said the numerous Federal Government projects being executed in Imo State demonstrate the benefits of President Bola Tinubu’s decision to remove fuel subsidy. The post Multiple projects in Imo justify fuel subsidy removal – Presidency appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Global Investors Hunt For Creative Backdoors Into China’s Mega CXMT IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Global investors largely shut out of China’s biggest IPO in nearly four years are looking at creative proxy trades as a way to profit from the country’s fast-rising memory-chip leader.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Daniel Abia, Port Harcourt Vice President Kashim Shettima has launched the Niger Delta Agricultural Development and Investment Fund and inaugurated a Coordinating Council for agricultural development and investment in the region. Speaking at the Niger Delta Agricultural Development and Investment

item.title: 'That's why he's the king' - Messi sets up third World Cup final
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: BBC Sport looks at Lionel Messi's masterclass on the right wing to knock England out of the World Cup.

item.title: 'He was my biggest fan' - BBC pundit Richards' father dies
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Micah Richards pays tribute to his father Lincoln, who died shortly before the BBC pundit went on air for England's World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The banner, held up by Lisandro Martinez and Giovani Lo Celso, goes against FIFA’s Stadium Code of Conduct.

item.title: IATA rolls out fresh guidelines for in-cabin pet travel
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has released new operational guidance to support airlines in managing in-cabin pet travel at every stage of the journey from booking to check-in, boarding, the on-board experience, and arrival. item.title: Kane: Holding on was not enough against Argentina
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England captain Harry Kane admitted trying to hold on to a 1-0 lead backfired as their World Cup hopes were ended by Argentina's late show in a 2-1 semi-final defeat.

item.title: NACCIMA, Chinese Partners Sign MoU To Drive FDI, Boost Nigeria-China Trade
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Association of Chambers of Commerce, Industry, Mines and Agriculture (NACCIMA) has signed a tripartite Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with China’s Linyi Wholesalers Federation and TTC Hydra Limited to deepen bilateral trade and facilitate fresh Chinese investments into Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: IN Nigeria’s legal profession, few distinctions command as much respect. item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Iran war, and its disruptions across global energy markets, is spurring investments for new US liquefied natural gas export infrastructure, according to consulting firm S&P Global.

item.title: ‘I don’t want to sound callous’: Why base your Social Security claim on life expectancy? We could die at any time.

item.description: “The actual date of death ultimately affects only the surviving beneficiaries.”

item.title: Li Ka-shing’s Cash-Out Plan Sets Up Empire for Leadership Shift
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Billionaire Li Ka-shing and his family are preparing to usher in a new generation of leaders after some of their longest-serving lieutenants wrap up the sale of various assets to overhaul Hong Kong’s largest conglomerate.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Soft Inflation, War Risk Cloud Fed Rate Outlook
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold steadied, as traders weighed softer-than-expected inflation data against escalating attacks in the Middle East for clues to the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: BHP Group Ltd.’s iron ore production fell in the three months through June as the miner pushes ahead with expansions in copper and potash.

item.title: Natural ways to keep black ants out of your sugar
item.dc:creator: Tribune

item.title: Senate Considers Nat’l Honours To Lawmakers’ Attendance, Performance
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Senate is considering tying national honours and official recognition to lawmakers’ attendance and participation in plenary proceedings. The proposal was made on Wednesday by Senator Abdul Ningi, PDP, Bauchi Central, during Wednesday’s plenary. He urged the Senate leadership to keep detail

item.title: Court dissolves APC’s chapters in UK, foreign countries, orders disclosure of donations from abroad
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The court added that the APC or any political party must notify INEC and account for all donations received from outside Nigeria within 21 days, in compliance with the Nigerian constitution. The post Court dissolves APC’s chapters in UK, foreign countries, orders disclosure of donations from a

item.title: You are missing the bond deal of the decade — and it is guaranteed to beat inflation
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: TIPS are offering a rare gift — and it is time to lock in a generous payout.

item.title: Liverpool open fresh talks to sign PSG’s Barcola
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Liverpool have stepped up their efforts to sign Paris Saint-Germain forward Bradley Barcola this summer.

item.title: Small-cap outperformance is persisting — and these 15 quality stocks pay rich dividends
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Think large caps are still the best bet? The data say you are looking in the wrong place.

item.title: Airtel Africa secures legal authorisation over planned boost of future shareholder returns
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The capital reduction entails cancelling Airtel Africa’s capital redemption reserve to boost distributable reserves, which are vital to the corporation’s prospects of delivering future rewards to shareholders. The post Airtel Africa secures legal authorisation over planned boost of future shareholde

item.title: Valve says iFixit will keep selling Steam Deck batteries after all
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Valve has been incredibly friendly to customers who need repairs - which is why it was so surprising to hear that Valve was already discontinuing the battery for the Steam Deck LCD handheld. It would have meant you could no longer just buy a battery from iFixit and install it yourself. Why would Val
